Commercial Finance Frequently Asked Questions

Comprehensive answers to common questions about equipment finance, commercial property, working capital, and business finance solutions for Australian commercial trades and civil contractors.

Industry Questions

Trade-specific finance

Equipment Finance Questions

What types of equipment can I finance through EVFBS?

We finance a comprehensive range of commercial equipment for established trades and contractors:

  • Heavy Machinery: Excavators, cranes, bulldozers, earthmoving equipment, concrete pumps
  • Commercial Vehicles: Utes, trucks, vans, trailers, specialised service vehicles
  • Trade Equipment: Welding machinery, HVAC equipment, electrical testing tools, workshop equipment
  • Specialised Equipment: Mining equipment, agricultural machinery, medical equipment, IT infrastructure

Finance amounts range from $20K to $500K+ with flexible terms from 1-7 years.

How quickly can I get equipment finance approval?

For established commercial businesses with complete documentation, we typically provide:

  • Initial Assessment: Within 2-4 hours of application
  • Approval Decision: 24-48 hours for straightforward applications
  • Settlement: 5-10 business days depending on equipment verification

Urgent equipment acquisitions can often be expedited with our priority service for time-sensitive projects.

What are the typical interest rates for equipment finance?

Commercial equipment finance rates vary based on several factors:

  • Typical Range: 6% to 15% per annum
  • Prime Commercial: 6-9% for strong financials and new equipment
  • Standard Commercial: 9-12% for established businesses
  • Specialist Finance: 12-15% for unique situations or older equipment

Rates depend on business financial strength, equipment type, loan term, and security offered. We negotiate competitive rates through our specialist lender network.

Should I choose equipment loan, lease, or hire purchase?

Each option offers different benefits for commercial businesses:

  • Equipment Loan: Immediate ownership, full depreciation benefits, equipment as security
  • Equipment Lease: Lower payments, 100% tax deductible, upgrade options, operational expense
  • Hire Purchase: Fixed payments, balloon options, ownership at completion, GST benefits

Our specialists will recommend the optimal structure based on your cash flow, tax position, and business objectives.

Can I finance used or second-hand equipment?

Yes, we regularly finance quality used equipment with specific considerations:

  • Age Limits: Typically equipment under 10 years old (varies by type)
  • Condition Assessment: Professional evaluation may be required
  • Finance Terms: Usually shorter terms (2-5 years) for used equipment
  • Higher Deposit: May require 10-20% deposit depending on age and condition

Quality used equipment from reputable dealers often qualifies for competitive rates.

Commercial Property Finance Questions

What commercial properties can I finance for my business?

We specialise in commercial property finance for:

  • Business Premises: Warehouses, workshops, offices, retail spaces
  • Industrial Properties: Manufacturing facilities, storage, distribution centres
  • Investment Properties: Commercial real estate for rental income
  • Development Projects: Commercial development and construction finance

Finance amounts from $100K to $5M+ with terms from 5-30 years depending on property type and use.

What deposit is required for commercial property finance?

Deposit requirements vary by property type and use:

  • Owner-Occupier: 10-20% for established businesses purchasing premises
  • Investment Property: 20-30% for commercial investment purchases
  • Development Finance: 20-40% depending on project type and experience
  • Specialised Properties: 30-50% for unique or limited-use properties

Existing business equity and equipment can sometimes be used as additional security to reduce cash deposit requirements.

How long does commercial property finance approval take?

Commercial property approval timeframes typically include:

  • Pre-Approval: 2-5 business days with complete documentation
  • Formal Approval: 10-15 business days after property valuation
  • Settlement: 30-45 days from contract exchange
  • Complex Deals: 6-8 weeks for development or unique properties

Having pre-approval strengthens your position when negotiating property purchases.

Can I use commercial property equity for business growth?

Yes, commercial property equity can be leveraged for business expansion:

  • Equipment Finance: Property equity as additional security for lower rates
  • Working Capital: Business line of credit secured by property
  • Business Expansion: Funding for new locations or business diversification
  • Refinancing: Access equity while securing better terms

We structure solutions to optimise tax benefits and maintain healthy debt service ratios.

Working Capital & Cash Flow Questions

What working capital solutions are available for commercial trades?

We offer flexible working capital solutions tailored to commercial trades:

  • Business Line of Credit: $50K-$500K revolving credit for operational flexibility
  • Invoice Factoring: Convert invoices to immediate cash (80-95% advance)
  • Seasonal Funding: Support for peak demand periods and project cycles
  • Bridging Finance: Short-term funding for payment delays or opportunities

Solutions are structured to match your business cash flow patterns and industry cycles.

How does a business line of credit work?

A business line of credit provides flexible access to working capital:

  • Credit Limit: Approved limit (e.g., $100K) that you can draw against as needed
  • Interest Charges: Pay interest only on amounts drawn, not the full limit
  • Repayment: Flexible repayments with ability to redraw
  • Security: Can be secured by property, equipment, or business assets

Ideal for managing cash flow gaps, taking advantage of supplier discounts, or funding growth opportunities.

What are the qualification criteria for working capital finance?

Working capital qualification typically requires:

  • Business Operation: Minimum 12 months trading history
  • Financial Performance: Positive cash flow and sustainable business model
  • Credit History: Acceptable business and director credit profiles
  • Documentation: Current financial statements and business plans

Established businesses with consistent performance typically qualify for competitive rates and terms.

Invoice Financing Questions

How does invoice financing work for commercial trades?

Invoice financing provides immediate cash from unpaid commercial invoices:

  • Initial Advance: Receive 80-95% of invoice value within 24 hours
  • Client Payment: Your clients pay invoices normally (often confidential)
  • Final Payment: Receive balance less fees when client pays
  • Non-Recourse Options: Protection against client default available

Particularly effective for businesses with 30-90 day payment terms from commercial clients.

What types of invoices qualify for financing?

We finance invoices from creditworthy commercial clients:

  • B2B Invoices: Business-to-business transactions with commercial clients
  • Government Work: Local, state, and federal government contracts
  • Large Corporates: ASX-listed companies and major corporations
  • Established SMEs: Medium-sized businesses with good payment history

Consumer invoices and residential work generally don't qualify for commercial invoice financing.

What are the costs associated with invoice financing?

Invoice financing costs typically include:

  • Factoring Fee: 1.5-4% of invoice value depending on client quality
  • Interest Charges: Monthly interest on advances (1-3% per month)
  • Setup Fees: $500-$2000 for initial due diligence and setup
  • Management Fees: Ongoing account management (often included)

Total cost typically ranges from 15-25% annually, often justified by improved cash flow and growth opportunities.

Application Process Questions

What documents do I need for a commercial finance application?

Required documentation varies by finance type but typically includes:

  • Financial Statements: 2 years business financials (profit & loss, balance sheet)
  • BAS Statements: Recent quarterly BAS lodgements
  • Bank Statements: 6 months business and personal statements
  • Equipment Documentation: Quotes, invoices, or valuations
  • Business Registration: ABN, ACN, business licences
  • Director Identification: Licence, passport, or proof of identity

We provide a comprehensive checklist during initial consultation to streamline the process.

Do you work with businesses that have imperfect credit history?

Yes, we specialise in finding solutions for businesses with various credit situations:

  • Credit Impairments: Defaults, late payments, or judgements
  • Previous Bankruptcies: Discharged bankruptcies with current strong performance
  • New Business: Limited credit history but strong business fundamentals
  • Industry Challenges: Businesses affected by economic conditions

Our extensive lender network includes specialists who focus on business performance and security rather than just credit scores.

How much can I borrow for my business?

Borrowing capacity depends on multiple factors:

  • Business Cash Flow: Ability to service debt comfortably
  • Security Available: Property, equipment, or business assets
  • Business Strength: Financial performance and stability
  • Industry Factors: Sector-specific lending criteria

General guidelines: Equipment finance $20K-$500K+, Property finance $100K-$5M+, Working capital $50K-$1M+. We provide detailed capacity assessments during consultation.

What security is required for commercial finance?

Security requirements vary by loan type and amount:

  • Equipment Finance: Equipment itself plus business guarantees
  • Property Finance: Property mortgage plus business guarantees
  • Working Capital: Business assets, property, or equipment
  • Unsecured Options: Limited amounts based on strong financials

Personal guarantees from directors are standard, but personal property security depends on loan size and risk assessment.

Industry-Specific Questions

Do you specialise in finance for commercial trades and contractors?

Yes, we specialise in finance for commercial trades and civil contractors:

  • Commercial Trades: Electricians, plumbers, HVAC, cleaning, security services
  • Civil Contractors: Earthmoving, excavation, road construction, utilities
  • Transport & Logistics: Freight, courier services, warehousing
  • Manufacturing: Light manufacturing, fabrication, processing

We understand industry-specific challenges including seasonal cash flows, project-based income, and equipment requirements.

Why don't you work with residential builders?

We focus on commercial sectors to provide specialised expertise:

  • Industry Focus: Deeper expertise in commercial and industrial sectors
  • Risk Management: Commercial trades typically have more stable cash flows
  • Specialised Solutions: Better equipped to serve established commercial businesses
  • Lender Relationships: Stronger partnerships with commercial finance specialists

This focus allows us to provide better outcomes for our target client base of established commercial businesses.

How do you handle seasonal businesses or project-based income?

We understand seasonal and project-based business models:

  • Seasonal Payment Plans: Repayment schedules aligned with income cycles
  • Line of Credit: Flexible access during lean periods
  • Project Finance: Funding tied to specific contracts or projects
  • Annual Assessment: Evaluate performance over full business cycles

We structure finance solutions to match your business patterns rather than forcing standard monthly payments.

What compliance and regulatory considerations apply?

We ensure full compliance with Australian finance regulations:

  • NCCP Compliance: Full National Consumer Credit Protection compliance
  • ASIC Licensed: Australian Credit Licence (ACL) authorisation
  • Responsible Lending: Comprehensive affordability assessments
  • Privacy Protection: Strict data protection and privacy protocols

All finance products are provided through licensed Australian lenders with appropriate regulatory protections.

Still Have Questions?

Our commercial finance specialists are here to provide personalised answers and professional guidance.
